mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in real savings indicate unsustainable demand pressures that force central banks to tighten, depressing long-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative predictive power (Rank IC < -0.02) over 60-day horizons in 3-year rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rebound in industrial production following a contraction phase reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside post-shock recovery regimes or fails to beat baseline in regime-stratified tests.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate crossing above 3.5 percent triggers immediate repricing of growth equity valuations due to higher borrowing costs and compressed duration premiums.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite rate crossing threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand without overheating, supporting sustained corporate revenue growth and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term yields negative or null predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in 3-year rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth rates signal weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate profit expectations, generating negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative during GDP deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index falling below 100 indicates severe supply-side contraction that disrupts manufacturing output and triggers immediate short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansion phases.
